item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: 2805-new.png
Author(s): National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), faminepulse
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by faminepulse as an edited version of the image below

Source: Flickr
License: Public Domain
Title: Walt Disney and Dr. Wernher von Braun
Author: National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It may not be the best article in the site, but it sure has the best play on words in the site. Kudos to you, kind sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i was not expecting this SCP to be this good, easy +1.

also i love how yoricspungement is a word here in the forums.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off i know I'm necroposting. The reason I'm doing so as a reply is because reading Vmkid's post brought up an idea that might contribute something to the discussion of this SCP. As Vmkid noted, there are factual mistakes. There are others, for example Walt Disney as dreamer not hard nosed businessman. Even the frozen head falls into this category. But unless I missed something, everyone is treating this (in universe of course) as the actual frozen head actually calling people.

But what if this SCP has nothing to do with the actual Walt Disney, even in universe? This is a Oneiroi Collective related SCP. All of these mistakes, and even some cheese that cheesed off some readers, all relate to what is popularly believed. What if this item was created by a Oneiroi hive mind focusing the fervent beliefs and dreams about Walk Disney from tens of millions of people? Or maybe it's the physical manifestation of a Oneiroi hive mind focused on Walt Disney. Sure I'm ret-coning, but it would seem a reasonable one and it explains the errors. This could be about what millions of people believe about Walt Disney, not about the actual person Walk Disney.

item.title: Re: Really dislike redacted in the middle, without context of removal
item.content:encoded: It's a stylistic choice, called a Yoricspungement

item.title: Re: Really dislike redacted in the middle, without context of removal
item.content:encoded: This bothers me also.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty funny skip, but the ending also hits hard.

+1

item.title: Congrats on +200!
item.content:encoded: This was pretty fun, happy to push it over a milestone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A small nitpick I have about this one is that Walt Disney World isn't a theme park. It's a resort. Epcot IS a theme park, though.

A much bigger nitpick is that Walt Disney never actually said "If you can dream it, you can do it."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah it's pretty awesome. Spot-on "Walt the Dreamer".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sure death mellowed him out a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hid[REDACTED]ribbean really irks me, but otherwise I really like this, especially the frozen insects thing. I however felt like the agent didn't really interwiew the SCP or ask any questions. Also, this is a simple cognitohazard that doesn't do anything unless it's looked at. Why is it Euclid?

Nonetheless, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Belated, but I've taken this advice and cut everything recommended to be cut. I've added a new paragraph in its place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thinking of the upcoming Tomorrowland movie as I reread this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was one of the first oneiroi articles.

Even better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was one of the first oneiroi articles. I'll update this later with the right cryothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. You managed to write an urban legend well, you used a smaller GoI, and the call transcript was great. On a minor note cryogenics is the study of materials at low temperatures, while cryonics would be preserving Walt Disney's head at low temperatures.

item.title: Re: Really dislike redacted in the middle, without context of removal
item.content:encoded: Especially since it still leaves enough information to make it really simple to fill in the blanks, thereby making the redaction completely pointless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-2805 is the severed head of American industrialist Walt Disney, frozen in a cryogenic freezer."

Aaaaand downvoted.

*Reads the rest of the article*

Aaaand still downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Went in this expecting something completely different, saw the picture, and it got better from there.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree wholeheartedly with this and I couldn't say it better. It's a great concept but a few elements make it cross the line between weird and cheesy.

item.title: Really dislike redacted in the middle, without context of removal
item.content:encoded: This part: "and SCP-2805 was discovered within a hid[REDACTED]ribbean." makes no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love everything about this. Upvote +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The play on words… SO CLEVER.
Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a huge Disney nerd. I gotta say, I really enjoyed this. I found it interesting and a touch unsettling.

+1 and nice job Roget. Also, for those who don't know, this is Roget's 100th article. Congrats on that as well!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really wanted to like this. You had my interest with the severed head of Walt Disney that makes phone calls to people that see it. That's the blend of bizarre and vaguely creepy that I love to see on the site, and the idea definitely had some moves. And the photoshop was a nice touch.

So the phone calls turn out to be Walt Disney and his excitement about the Epcot Center, and I'm like "yes, this is surreal, weird, and not obvious", and I head firmly into upvote territory.

Then I get to the part about it making you dream about the whole Epcot thing, and I'm wondering why it's there, but it doesn't really kill it for me yet. I didn't care for the too-easy Fascist Disney reference in the second paragraph.

And then I get to the parts where it's a Thing What Compels You. And what was a beautifully strange, vaguely unsettling thing that I loved so much turned into yet another object with an agenda that uses the most obvious possible means to achieve it. And a significant percentage of Disney World employees are affected by this? Really? Did they parade Walt Disney's head around in public? How would that work? And why did the secretaries think nothing of seeing the frozen head of Walt Disney in some crazy Russian contraption, but then report the phone calls?

SCP-2805 was discovered within a hid[REDACTED]ribbean.

Oh come on.

I would have upvoted this without hesitation if the description had stopped after the part where Walt Disney calls you and it went straight to the wonderfully weird phone call log. But that middle part absolutely wrecked it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one that read that agent's dialogue in the voice of Hugo Weaving?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ 1

Nothing else to say, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, it's not just Walt's cryogenically frozen head, it's Walt's cryogenically frozen head while he still dreams of his Great Big Beautiful Tomorrow.

Utterly fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The difference is, no one outside of the Foundation is supposed to know about it. Everyone knows the CIA exists, they just don't know the specifics in what they do.

If former employees of the Foundation were tracked and found, and people found there was a giant organization holding thousands of dangerous artifacts and creatures, the shitstorm would be a lot worse than some controversial CIA methods.

Could people retire? Probably, if they've done enough. Definitely not without amnestics, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The size of the Foundation requires it to employ shitloads of people. People who get partial glimpses of Weird Shit. It's unworkable to say nobody leaves without amnestics or death.

I mean, beyond the fact that nobody apparently ever thinks of the logistics of anything, think of it this way: the CIA lets people leave, the CIA believes its work to be as secret and as important as the Foundation does, and the CIA is also blatantly evil and totally psychotic. If the maniacs at the CIA can let people resign, the Foundation can do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The next of kin will receive the call, with Walt's condolences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if the person is killed before receiving a call?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Still gets a call

Walt would probably get confused and hang up if they didn't respond

If they know people with phones, he'd call one of them and ask for the person to be put on the line. If not, the next time they're near a phone, it's for them.

Similar to answer 2

item.title: HEY LOOK, FREE SHOOPS
item.content:encoded:
picture looks quite real

Thanks! I try.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thinking on it, that's probably a shout-out to Ed's Head, isn't it. Clever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I noticed that and was very amused to see his lazy censor make a comeback. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
" SCP-2805 is the severed head of American industrialist Walt Disney, frozen in a cryogenic freezer."

This was the first sentence I read in this article, and it made me intensely happy. The article as a whole is good too. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the last bit is yoricspungement

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. I just have a few minor nits to pick.

Digital analysis has confirmed that the voice on the other end of the line sounds identical to recordings of Walt's voice circa 1965.

I agree with Kapitano here. Instead of "sounds identical to" maybe just go with "matches". Also, referring to the subject by just his first name could be considered a tone issue. Unless the intent was that the researcher who wrote the description was under the influence of the object…

SCP-2805: Marty. I hired him to work the newspaper, and after my, uh, illness, which I shall not go into, he was placed in charge of the Florida project. That's what I called it. But, they couldn't do it. All my imagineers, brilliant men, but they couldn't do it.

SCP-2805: The vision. None of them grasped it. I had thought, with the film, it was very explicit, but it didn't come across to them. I wanted a community of tomorrow, but they gave me a world's fair. Sklar, Marty, he was a wonderful boy, but he got it wrong.

Maybe it's just me, but "Sklar" in the second paragraph seemed like a typo. I had to Google it to realize it was Marty's last name. Maybe go with "Marty Sklar. I hired him to work the newspaper" and "Marty, he was a wonderful boy"

Also wouldn't it be "hid████████████████████████████████ribbean" instead of "hid[REDACTED]ribbean" if it's only a partial sentence being redacted?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot. The picture looks quite real and the interview log is somehow both sweet and sinister.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: credit to famine for being the king of shoopy woopy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Roget directed me to add the picture on his behalf, and so I have done this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was a little iffy at first on using Walt Disney, but I rather like the interview and the package as a whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amnesticized, I believe. Not sure, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had the same thought, but people must do it. Though I imagine they're amnestified when they do.

Amnesified?

Amnesticed?

Probably some O5 members would rather everyone who retired was quietly Terminated afterwards. Maybe that happens when people go awol or run away from the Foundation.

I'd like to think there's something like Patrick McGoohan's Villiage somewhere for retired agents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a remark, not a criticism: I never imagined people being able to leave the employ of the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Digital analysis has confirmed that the voice on the other end of the line sounds identical to recordings of Walt's voice circa 1965

So a computer thinks it seems to be exactly the same as Walt Disney? Either a computer finds no difference in voice patterns, or a person thinks the voice sounds similar. It can't be both.

Automobiles are described as being crushed and used in construction, while those who did not like the policies were removed in a nondescript manner.

Ambiguous pronouns. "Those" presumably refers to people, but grammatically it's talking about cars. And, what is a "nondescript" manner for "removing" people?

Aside from that, I like the way you use the myth of Disney's frozen head. The "futuristic city" sounds like a modern take on the Lovecraftian city of incomprehensible aliens. It does make Uncle Walt sound like a confused dreamer though, rather than the hard nosed businessman he was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What happens if the person is blind and can't 'view' the head?

What happens if the person is deaf?

What happens if the person does not have a telephone?

What happens if the person does not speak or understand English?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No-voting because this version of Disney seems far too sanitized. It's like the Walt Disney company is intercepting the phone calls and forcing Walt to tone down his personality to become less like the real Walt Disney and more like the Saving Mr. Banks version of Walt Disney.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That ending…

+1
